title: tetracera hirsuta
intro: Tetracera hirsuta is a species of flowering plant in the family Tetracercaceae, commonly known as the 'caterpillar plant' due to its unique caterpillar-like appearance. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Tetracera hirsuta include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use in wound healing and skin conditions. The herbal preparations of Tetracera hirsuta include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be made by boiling the leaves, stems, and roots in water to extract its medicinal properties.
